prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial opens in a very dark bedroom at night. A bed fills the foreground, with at least one person lying under bedding; the details of the face and body are hard to distinguish because of the low light. A row of small string lights is visible across the window area in the background, giving the room a dim, warm outline.

By the next sampled frame, much later in the clip, the setting has shifted to a softly lit home interior. A man is using a stationary exercise bike positioned near a window with lights along the frame. He is seated on the bike and turned slightly toward a large attached screen in front of him. Through an open doorway behind him, another room is visible, including a decorated Christmas tree with lights. The room appears domestic, with curtains, framed wall art, and furniture visible.

In the final frame, the man is shown more clearly in profile riding the exercise bike near the window. The Christmas tree remains visible in the background through the doorway. A large white “PELOTON” logo is superimposed across the center of the image, with the Peloton icon to the left. Small text reading “OnePeloton.com” appears near the bottom center. The final composition emphasizes the exercise bike, the rider, and the brand identity in a home setting.
